0x01.name: APC_INDEX_MISMATCH
0x01.cause: Kernel APC index mismatch - usually a driver or filesystem filter

0x0A.name: IRQL_NOT_LESS_OR_EQUAL
0x0A.cause: Driver accessed paged memory at wrong IRQL level
0x0A.usually_not: ntoskrnl.exe is usually NOT root cause - it is the detector
check: lm t n for driver timestamps

0x0D.name: MUTEX_LEVEL_NUMBER_VIOLATION
0x0D.cause: Driver acquired mutex at wrong IRQL level
check: Check which driver called the lock operation in stack

0x19.name: BAD_POOL_HEADER
0x19.cause: Pool header corruption - driver wrote past end of pool allocation or freed pool twice
check: Driver Verifier Special Pool on suspect drivers

0x1A.name: MEMORY_MANAGEMENT
0x1A.cause: Memory manager detected critical error - driver corrupted MM structures
0x1A.usually_not: ntoskrnl.exe. Check third-party filter drivers (file system, network, display)
check: Compare dump driver list vs disk drivers

0x1E.name: KMODE_EXCEPTION_NOT_HANDLED
0x1E.cause: Kernel-mode code raised an unhandled exception
0x1E.usually_not: ntoskrnl.exe. Non-Microsoft drivers in the stack are primary suspects

0x24.name: NTFS_FILE_SYSTEM
0x24.cause: NTFS detected corruption in its own data structures - usually disk or filter driver
0x24.usually_not: Ntfs.sys itself unless disk has bad sectors

0x3B.name: SYSTEM_SERVICE_EXCEPTION
0x3B.cause: Exception during system service - usually driver caused

0x3D.name: INTERRUPT_EXCEPTION_NOT_HANDLED
0x3D.cause: CPU received interrupt it couldn't handle - usually device driver or overclocking

0x4E.name: PFN_LIST_CORRUPT
0x4E.cause: Page Frame Number database corrupted - driver wrote to wrong physical page
0x4E.usually_not: ntoskrnl.exe. Memory corruption from driver or bad RAM

0x50.name: PAGE_FAULT_IN_NONPAGED_AREA
0x50.usually_not: ntoskrnl.exe. Check if fault address points to freed memory

0x6B.name: PROCESS1_INITIALIZATION_FAILED
0x6B.cause: Boot-critical process failed to start - Bootcat.cache, NTFS, or disk issue
check: Boot from recovery media and repair boot files

0x74.name: BAD_SYSTEM_CONFIG_INFO
0x74.cause: Registry hive or boot configuration data is corrupt
check: Check for recently installed software that modified registry

0x77.name: KERNEL_STACK_INPAGE_ERROR
0x77.cause: Kernel stack page could not be read from disk - disk or controller failure

0x7A.name: KERNEL_DATA_INPAGE_ERROR
0x7A.cause: Kernel data could not be read from pagefile - disk or storage controller

0x7B.name: INACCESSIBLE_BOOT_DEVICE
0x7B.cause: Windows cannot access the system disk during boot - storage driver or BIOS
check: Boot from recovery and check disk access

0x7E.name: SYSTEM_THREAD_EXCEPTION_NOT_HANDLED
0x7E.cause: Unhandled exception in a system thread

0x9C.name: MACHINE_CHECK_EXCEPTION
0x9C.cause: CPU detected fatal hardware error. Outside skill scope - pure hardware.

0x9F.name: DRIVER_POWER_STATE_FAILURE
0x9F.cause: Driver failed during power state transition - sleep/wake/shutdown

0xA0.name: INTERNAL_POWER_ERROR
0xA0.cause: Power policy manager detected inconsistency - usually power/sleep driver

0xBE.name: ATTEMPTED_WRITE_TO_READONLY_MEMORY
0xBE.cause: Driver attempted to write to read-only memory region
0xBE.usually_not: The driver performing the write is usually the culprit

0xC1.name: SPECIAL_POOL_DETECTED_MEMORY_CORRUPTION
0xC1.cause: Driver Verifier Special Pool caught driver writing past pool boundary
0xC1.usually_not: The driver that allocated the pool (check !poolval)
check: Driver Verifier is active - this is GOOD, it caught the culprit
Check which driver is being verified
The pool tag in the dump identifies the offending driver

0xC2.name: BAD_POOL_CALLER
0xC2.cause: Thread made a bad kernel memory pool request

arg1_meanings.0x0d: Release quota on corrupted pool allocation - another driver wrote bad data

0xC2.usually_not: Ntfs.sys / ntoskrnl.exe are usually NOT root cause. NTFS detected corruption by someone else. Decode Arg3 hex to ASCII to find source driver's pool tag.
check: Compare dump vs disk driver list

0xC4.name: DRIVER_VERIFIER_DETECTED_VIOLATION
0xC4.cause: Driver Verifier caught a driver breaking kernel rules - this is GOOD
0xC4.usually_not: Driver Verifier is the detector. The offending driver is named in the dump.
check: Read the dump - it names the exact driver
If stable after fix, disable Verifier: verifier /reset

0xC5.name: DRIVER_CORRUPTED_EXPOOL
0xC5.cause: Driver corrupted system pool that was already freed - double-free or use-after-free

0xC9.name: DRIVER_VERIFIER_IOMANAGER_VIOLATION
0xC9.cause: Driver Verifier caught a driver misusing I/O manager APIs

0xCC.name: PAGE_FAULT_IN_FREED_SPECIAL_POOL
0xCC.cause: Driver accessed memory that was freed and reallocated to Special Pool
check: This is a use-after-free in a driver
Driver Verifier Special Pool caught it

0xCE.name: DRIVER_UNLOADED_WITHOUT_CANCELLING_PENDING_OPERATIONS
0xCE.cause: Driver was unloaded while it still had pending I/O operations
check: The unloaded driver is named in the dump
Related to hot-plug or software uninstall while active

0xD1.name: DRIVER_IRQL_NOT_LESS_OR_EQUAL
0xD1.cause: Driver accessed paged memory at too high IRQL

0xD5.name: DRIVER_PAGE_FAULT_IN_FREED_SPECIAL_POOL
0xD5.cause: Driver accessed freed Special Pool memory - use-after-free
check: Driver Verifier Special Pool was active
Dump names the driver that accessed freed memory

0xD6.name: DRIVER_PAGE_FAULT_BEYOND_END_OF_ALLOCATION
0xD6.cause: Driver accessed memory past the end of its pool allocation
check: Pool overflow - the driver that owns the allocation
Driver Verifier Special Pool catches this

0xDA.name: SYSTEM_PTE_MISUSE
0xDA.cause: Kernel Page Table Entry misuse - driver or memory corruption
check: Check for driver that heavily uses virtual memory

0xDC.name: DRIVER_INVALID_STACK_ACCESS
0xDC.cause: Driver accessed stack memory that doesn't belong to it
check: Possible stack buffer overflow in a driver

0xEA.name: THREAD_STUCK_IN_DEVICE_DRIVER
0xEA.cause: GPU driver stuck in infinite loop - display driver issue

0xED.name: UNMOUNTABLE_BOOT_VOLUME
0xED.cause: Windows cannot mount the boot disk - disk, filesystem, or storage driver
check: Check storage driver version (especially after Windows Update)

0xEF.name: CRITICAL_PROCESS_DIED
0xEF.cause: A critical Windows process (csrss.exe, wininit.exe, etc.) was terminated
check: Event log for which process died

0xF4.name: CRITICAL_OBJECT_TERMINATION
0xF4.cause: A critical system object (process or thread) was unexpectedly terminated

0xF5.name: FLTMGR_FILE_SYSTEM_FILTER
0xF5.cause: File system filter manager detected fatal error - filter driver problem
check: fltmc filters - list all filesystem filters

0xF7.name: DRIVER_OVERRAN_STACK_BUFFER
0xF7.cause: Driver wrote past its stack buffer - stack buffer overflow in kernel
0xF7.usually_not: The driver that overran its stack
check: Update or remove the offending driver

0xFC.name: ATTEMPTED_EXECUTE_OF_NOEXECUTE_MEMORY
0xFC.cause: CPU tried to execute code from non-executable memory - driver bug or exploit attempt

0xFE.name: BUGCODE_USB_DRIVER
0xFE.cause: USB driver detected an internal error
check: Disconnect all USB devices and test
Check for USB device firmware updates

0x101.name: CLOCK_WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT
0x101.cause: CPU clock interrupt was not received - CPU/overclocking/BIOS
0x101.usually_not: Can be driver if driver disabled interrupts too long

0x109.name: CRITICAL_STRUCTURE_CORRUPTION
0x109.cause: Kernel detected critical data structure was overwritten - driver wrote to wrong memory
check: Check for kernel-mode drivers writing to wrong addresses

0x10D.name: WDF_VIOLATION
0x10D.cause: Windows Driver Framework detected a driver violating WDF rules
check: Dump names the violating WDF driver

0x10E.name: VIDEO_MEMORY_MANAGEMENT_INTERNAL
0x10E.cause: Video memory manager internal error - GPU driver or VRAM

0x113.name: VIDEO_DXGKRNL_FATAL_ERROR
0x113.cause: DirectX graphics kernel fatal error - GPU driver crashed the kernel
check: GPU driver clean reinstall with DDU

0x116.name: VIDEO_TDR_FAILURE
0x116.cause: GPU timeout and reset - display driver issue

0x117.name: VIDEO_TDR_EXTENSION
0x117.cause: Extended GPU timeout - display driver couldn't recover
check: Same as 0x116 plus: check GPU power delivery
Clean install GPU driver with DDU
Test with different GPU driver version

0x119.name: VIDEO_SCHEDULER_INTERNAL_ERROR
0x119.cause: GPU scheduler violation - display driver issue

0x122.name: WHEA_INTERNAL_ERROR
0x122.cause: Windows Hardware Error Architecture internal error

0x124.name: WHEA_UNCORRECTABLE_ERROR
0x124.cause: Hardware error - CPU/PCIe. NOT a driver problem.
0x124.usually_not: This is hardware. Outside skill scope.

0x126.name: NETIO_INVALID_POOL_CALLER
0x126.cause: Network I/O subsystem detected bad pool operation - network driver/LSP

0x133.name: DPC_WATCHDOG_VIOLATION
0x133.cause: DPC routine ran too long - storage or network driver during heavy I/O

0x139.name: KERNEL_SECURITY_CHECK_FAILURE
0x139.cause: Kernel detected corrupted data structure - driver corruption

0x13A.name: KERNEL_MODE_HEAP_CORRUPTION
0x13A.cause: Kernel-mode heap was corrupted - driver wrote to freed heap or buffer overflow

0x141.name: VIDEO_ENGINE_TIMEOUT_DETECTED
0x141.cause: GPU engine was stuck and reset - GPU driver or hardware

0x145.name: SECURE_BOOT_VIOLATION
0x145.cause: Secure Boot policy was violated - unsigned or tampered driver/bootloader
check: Disable Secure Boot temporarily to test

0x15E.name: NETADAPTER_VIOLATION
0x15E.cause: Network adapter driver framework violation - NDIS/WiFi/Ethernet driver

0x18B.name: SECURE_KERNEL_ERROR
0x18B.cause: Secure kernel error - known caused by KB5053656/KB5055523 on Win11 24H2
check: Check if KB5053656 or KB5055523 installed

0x1A8.name: VIDEO_DXGKRNL_LIVEDUMP
0x1A8.cause: GPU driver generated a live dump - non-fatal but should be investigated
